success_surface: Cell-level text matching with aggregation across multiple columns
effective_behaviors: Pre-execution data inspection to confirm cell structure and identify target ranges before coding
Precise scope extraction from natural language instructions mapping keywords to specific column ranges
Use of word boundary regex patterns for complete word matching instead of substring matching
Defensive null/None checking before accessing values in data rows
Case-insensitive matching when semantic equivalence is required
Explicit data type conversion for numeric aggregation operations
Preserving source file integrity by writing to separate output path
Post-execution result verification through printed summaries
evidence: Agent ran inspection code to print cell values before writing solution logic
Correctly parsed A2-A6, B2-B6, D2-D5, and E2:E5 ranges from instruction description
Used r'\b' + re.escape(root) + r'\b' pattern to enforce complete word boundaries
Included 'if root is None' and 'if keyword is None or volume is None' guards
Applied re.IGNORECASE flag in regex search
Converted volume values with int(volume) before summing
Saved to /output_path instead of overwriting source file
Printed results with row-by-row verification at end of execution

memory_items.title: Structural Verification Before Manipulation
memory_items.lesson: Always inspect spreadsheet structure programmatically before implementing changes
memory_items.generalization: Read sheet names, header cells, and sample row values to confirm data layout matches assumptions before executing modification code

memory_items.title: Word Boundary Matching for Text Comparison
memory_items.lesson: Use regex word boundaries when complete word matching is required
memory_items.generalization: Apply \\b anchors around search terms in regex patterns to prevent partial substring matches during text comparison tasks

memory_items.title: Defensive Value Retrieval Pattern
memory_items.lesson: Handle null and missing values explicitly during row iteration
memory_items.generalization: Check for None/null values before performing calculations or comparisons to prevent runtime errors

memory_items.title: Semantic Matching Considerations
memory_items.lesson: Apply case-insensitive matching when exact string identity is not semantically required
memory_items.generalization: Use case-insensitive flags in string comparison operations when matching should succeed despite capitalization differences

memory_items.title: Type Safety in Numeric Aggregation
memory_items.lesson: Convert string or mixed-type values to appropriate numeric types before arithmetic
memory_items.generalization: Explicitly cast values to int/float before summation or other mathematical operations to ensure type consistency

edits.content:
## 6. Trace-Derived Lessons
- **Structural Verification Before Manipulation**: Always inspect spreadsheet structure programmatically before implementing changes. Read sheet names, header cells, and sample row values to confirm data layout matches assumptions.
- **Word Boundary Matching for Text Comparison**: Apply \\\\b anchors around search terms in regex patterns to prevent partial substring matches during text comparison tasks requiring complete word matching.
- **Defensive Value Retrieval Pattern**: Check for None/null values explicitly before performing calculations or comparisons during row iteration to prevent runtime errors.
- **Semantic Matching Considerations**: Use case-insensitive flags in string comparison operations when exact string identity is not semantically required but meaning preservation is important.
- **Type Safety in Numeric Aggregation**: Explicitly cast values to int/float before summation or other mathematical operations to ensure type consistency and avoid concatenation bugs.
